days.title: Arrival in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant streets

timeline.title: Arrive at Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Land at Narita Airport. Follow the signs to the Airport Limousine Bus for a direct ride to Shibuya.

timeline.title: Check in to Shibuya Granbell Hotel
timeline.detail: Located just a 5-minute walk from Shibuya Station, this hotel offers modern comforts and a vibrant atmosphere.

timeline.title: Shibuya Scramble Crossing
timeline.detail: Experience the iconic Shibuya Crossing, known for its hustle and bustle. It's a great introduction to Tokyo's energy.

timeline.title: Dinner at Uobei Sushi
timeline.detail: Enjoy a unique sushi experience at this conveyor belt sushi restaurant where you can order directly from a tablet.

timeline.title: Explore Shibuya Nightlife
timeline.detail: Take a stroll around Shibuya's nightlife scene. Visit bars, karaoke spots, and enjoy the city lights.

localTips: Purchase a Suica card at the airport for easy train travel.
Brush up on basic Japanese phrases; locals appreciate attempts to communicate.
Visit the 109 shopping mall for trendy fashion during your stroll.
Stay hydrated! Convenience stores offer a wide variety of drinks and snacks.
Download a map app in case of spotty internet connection.

days.title: Tokyo City Highlights
days.subtitle: Temples, parks, and shopping

timeline.title: Breakfast at Hoshino Coffee
timeline.detail: Start your day with their specialty fluffy pancakes and signature coffee. A perfect pick-me-up for your adventures.

timeline.title: Senso-ji Temple Visit
timeline.detail: Explore Tokyo's oldest temple in Asakusa. Don't miss the impressive Kaminarimon Gate and Nakamise shopping street.

timeline.title: Lunch at Asakusa Nanze
timeline.detail: Enjoy traditional tempura and soba at this popular local eatery renowned for its high-quality dishes and affordability.

timeline.title: Ueno Park Stroll
timeline.detail: Walk through Ueno Park, visit local vendors, and enjoy the serene environment surrounded by cherry blossom trees.

timeline.title: Visit Tokyo National Museum
timeline.detail: Discover a vast collection of art and antiquities at Japan's oldest and largest museum located inside Ueno Park.
timeline.mapQuery: Tokyo National Museum, Ueno, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Ichiran Ramen
timeline.detail: Experience the unique dining concept of Ichiran, focusing on a single dish, tonkotsu ramen, prepared to perfection.

localTips: Purchase an art ticket if you plan to visit multiple museums; it can save you some money.
Try to visit Senso-ji early in the morning to avoid crowds.
Look for free guided tours at many museums and temples.
Do not miss the street food at Nakamise-dori leading to Senso-ji.
Take an umbrella in spring; rain showers can be unpredictable.

days.title: Cultural Exploration in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Learning about traditions and contemporary art

timeline.title: Breakfast at your hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a hearty breakfast spread at your hotel to fuel up for today's explorations.

timeline.title: Visit Meiji Shrine
timeline.detail: Stroll through the tranquil forested paths leading up to this Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken.

timeline.title: Harajuku Shopping
timeline.detail: Explore the colorful streets of Harajuku, known for its trendy shops, quirky fashion, and the iconic Takeshita Street.

timeline.title: Lunch at Kawaii Monster Cafe
timeline.detail: Immerse yourself in the whimsical world of this themed cafe, featuring colorful decor, and monster-inspired dishes.
timeline.mapQuery: Kawaii Monster Cafe, Harajuku, Tokyo, Japan

timeline.title: National Art Center Visit
timeline.detail: Visit this unique museum that showcases temporary exhibitions and highlights contemporary art without a permanent collection.
timeline.mapQuery: National Art Center Tokyo, Roppongi, Japan

timeline.title: Dinner at Gyukatsu Tokyo
timeline.detail: Savor the experience of gyukatsu, a breaded beef cutlet that you can grill yourself right at your table.

localTips: Check out the unique shops along Takeshita Street for exclusive Harajuku fashion.
Meiji Shrine has an entrance fee but is worth the visit for tranquility.
Fitting in is important; try to dress modestly at Meiji Shrine.
Take time to relax at Yoyogi Park after visiting Meiji Shrine.
The National Art Center has no permanent exhibitions; check what's on display before your visit.

days.title: Last Day in Tokyo
days.subtitle: Wrapping up and last-minute shopping

timeline.title: Breakfast at Paul Bakery
timeline.detail: Indulge in a selection of fresh pastries and coffee at this French-inspired bakery located in Tokyo Station.

timeline.title: Ginza Shopping District Visit
timeline.detail: Explore the upscale boutiques and flagship stores in Ginza, perfect for high-end shopping and discovering local brands.

timeline.title: Lunch at Sukiyabashi Jiro (reservation required)
timeline.detail: Experience world-renowned sushi at Sukiyabashi Jiro. Reservations are mandatory, book in advance to secure your spot.

timeline.title: Tokyo Tower Visit
timeline.detail: Take the elevator up to the observation deck of the iconic Tokyo Tower for breathtaking views of the city skyline.

timeline.title: Return to Narita Airport
timeline.detail: Head back to Narita Airport via Airport Limousine Bus for your departing flight. Allow enough time for travel and check-in.

highlights: Tokyo skyline views from Tokyo Tower
localTips: Sukiyabashi Jiro requires reservations weeks in advance; plan ahead.
Be sure to check the opening hours of shopping stores in Ginza.
Use your Suica card to make shopping at convenience stores easier.
Visit Tokyo Tower in the late afternoon to enjoy both daytime and sunset views.
Keep your passport handy for tax-free shopping at select stores.

hotels.name: Shibuya Granbell Hotel
hotels.note: 5-min walk from Shibuya Station, trendy decor.

budget.note: General entry fees and transport costs
Estimate for Suica top-up for 4 days

urgent.label: Book Sukiyabashi Jiro Lunch
urgent.note: Reservation required weeks in advance for this popular sushi restaurant.
